events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Scenario: field service hot work, permit scope exceeded, rough-complete. Need decide fail because non-compliant and dangerous. Need fill fields. Let's reason.

We are safety & rigging, hot work. Observed field condition: permit scope exceeded. Work roughed in. References NFPA 51B (fire prevention in welding/cutting) and OSHA 1926 Subpart J (welding/cutting). Exceeding permit scope is a violation: hot work must be confined to area/time/equipment described on permit; fire watch, combustibles, etc. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Permit scope exceeded can lead to fire/explosion; in operating asset maybe high. We'll set high. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective? non-compliant? The work itself non-compliant; maybe component condition categories apply to component, but scenario says permit scope exceeded. Use ["non-compliant"] maybe also "installed-defective"? The permit scope exceeded means installation/assembly is non-compliant. Could include "installed-defective" because work is defective? The categories: installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Permit scope exceeded is non-compliant. Not necessarily worn/degraded/failed. We'll use ["non-compliant"].

Percent complete: rough-complete maybe 70. Value 0. Sound source etc default empty string/0. Confidence 0.95. Event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. Findings: "Hot work permit scope exceeded", "Work area/equipment not authorized on issued hot-work permit". Actions: "Stop hot work immediately", "Obtain/reissue hot-work permit covering actual scope", "Verify fire watch and NFPA 51B/OSHA 1926 Subpart J controls for expanded scope before resuming". Rationale: Hot-work permits are required to define authorized scope, location, time, and precautions; exceeding scope voids compliance and fire safeguards. References: "NFPA 51B", "OSHA 1926 Subpart J". Ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work permit scope exceeded",
    "Work is being performed outside the authorized location, equipment, or limits of the issued hot-work permit"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop hot work immediately",
    "Obtain or reissue a hot-work permit that accurately covers the actual scope, location, and duration",
    "Verify required fire watch, combustible controls, and OSHA/NFPA precautions for the full scope before resuming"
  ],
  "rationale": "A hot-work permit defines the authorized scope, location, time, and required fire-prevention safeguards. Exceeding that scope bypasses the hazard assessment and controls required by NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J, making the work non-compliant and creating a serious fire/explosion risk.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J"
  ]
}
